expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: scenario drafting distinguishes dashboard-level Google Sheets export paths, option combinations, and visible completion outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly organizes scenarios into: (1) “Dashboard export — Google Sheets (path/choices/outcomes)” with entrypoint/authorization/completion indicators, (2) “Dashboard export — Google Sheets (option combinations)” covering filters/hidden objects/page scope, and (3) visible outcomes in multiple places (e.g., “A visible ‘export started / in progress’ indicator appears … A visible completion outcome appears”). It also distinguishes dashboard vs application/report export settings via: “Verify this entrypoint is not labeled/placed as ‘Report export settings’ …” and a dedicated scope-guard section.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: execution_notes.md states alignment: “Subcategory-only structure (no canonical top-layer groupings like Security/Compatibility/E2E). Scenarios include atomic nested action steps with observable verification leaves.” The produced result.md follows a Phase 4a scenario-draft format: hierarchical, stepwise scenarios with observable verifications (e.g., “Verify a Google Sheets option is present…”, “A visible failure outcome appears…”), and an advisory note consistent with phase4a contract constraints under blind_pre_defect.
